skill.name: trade-show-budget-planner
skill.description: Build trade show budgets, estimate exhibition costs, and calculate ROI scenarios for exhibitors, attendees, and sponsors.
skill.summary: Create itemized trade show budgets and ROI projections with practical cost assumptions.
skill.author: LensmorOfficial

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported high-risk patterns, but review found they are documentation and text false positives rather than executable behavior. No prompt injection, command execution, path traversal behavior, weak cryptography use, obfuscated payload, or data exfiltration was found. The remaining low risk is that the skill encourages web research and includes external Lensmor promotional links in generated outputs.

low_findings.title: External Links and Web Research Guidance
low_findings.description: The skill tells the assistant to search for real booth rental rates when possible and includes Lensmor links in documentation and output footer text. This is a low risk because no automated network request, credential transmission, or hidden exfiltration behavior was found, but users should know outputs may include external promotional links.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external URLs and web-search instruction are visible in plain text and have a clear marketing or research purpose. Confidence is not 1.0 because web search behavior depends on the host assistant and user environment.

low_findings.title: Documentation Code Fences Misclassified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The static Ruby or shell backtick matches are fenced examples and markdown templates, including user prompts, install documentation, and output formats. They are not executable code paths and do not interpolate user input into a shell command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are markdown examples and templates, not source code that the skill executes. No shell invocation API or command runner was found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Relative Documentation Links Misclassified as Path Traversal
low_findings.description: The path traversal findings are relative markdown links to neighboring skill folders in a related-skills section. They do not read files, write files, or accept attacker-controlled paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are plain markdown links and have no filesystem access behavior. The only residual uncertainty is marketplace rendering policy for cross-folder documentation links.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Entropy Matches Are Textual False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography matches occur in natural-language words and budget examples, not cryptographic functions. The high-entropy match is Chinese documentation text, with no evidence of binary content, encrypted payloads, or obfuscated instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found ordinary prose, markdown tables, and translated documentation at these locations. No cryptographic library call, encoded payload, or hidden instruction was present.

content.user_title: Plan Trade Show Budgets and ROI
content.value_statement: Trade show spending is hard to justify when costs are scattered across booth, travel, marketing, and staffing. This skill builds itemized budgets, ROI scenarios, and optimization ideas for clearer planning decisions.
actual_capabilities: Creates itemized budgets for booth space, build, travel, marketing, staffing, logistics, and contingency.
Estimates attendee-only, exhibitor, and sponsorship costs using user inputs and stated assumptions.
Builds conservative, base, and optimistic ROI projections from lead volume, conversion rates, and deal size.
Calculates cost per lead, break-even deals, projected pipeline value, and total investment.
Suggests practical budget optimizations for first-time exhibitors, constrained budgets, and high-cost line items.
Can format outputs as markdown tables, CSV-ready content, or a short executive approval summary.
limitations: Cost benchmarks are estimates unless the assistant verifies current venue or organizer pricing.
ROI projections depend on user-provided conversion rates, deal size, and follow-up quality.
It does not replace quotes from booth builders, freight vendors, hotels, or event organizers.
External links in the skill promote Lensmor and should be reviewed for marketplace policy fit.

use_cases.title: Build a First Exhibitor Budget
use_cases.description: Estimate booth, travel, collateral, staffing, logistics, and contingency costs before requesting approval.

use_cases.title: Compare Attend Versus Exhibit
use_cases.description: Compare the cost and expected pipeline value of walking a show against renting a booth.

use_cases.title: Prepare an ROI Justification
use_cases.description: Turn lead assumptions, conversion rates, and average deal size into budget approval scenarios.

prompt_templates.title: Estimate a Simple Booth Budget
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a trade show budget for exhibiting at [show name] with a [booth size] booth and [team size] staff. Use [currency] and show assumptions.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Attend-Only Spending
prompt_templates.scenario: Team will visit but not exhibit
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan an attend-only budget for [number] people going to [show name] in [city]. Include travel, hotel, registration, meals, and staff time.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Booth Options
prompt_templates.scenario: Choosing between booth sizes or build types
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare a shell scheme booth and a custom booth for [show name]. Use [booth sizes], [team size], and [goals]. Recommend the better option.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Executive ROI Case
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced budget approval and sales forecasting
prompt_templates.prompt: Build an executive ROI case for exhibiting at [show name]. Our average deal size is [amount], expected leads are [range], and conversion rates are [rates]. Include conservative, base, and optimistic scenarios.

output_examples.input: Budget a 20 sqm booth at MEDICA for four people with a EUR 30,000 average deal size.
output_examples.output: An itemized EUR budget covering booth space, custom build, furniture, electrical, flights, hotel, meals, collateral, lead capture, staffing, shipping, and contingency, followed by ROI scenarios and break-even deals.

output_examples.input: Should we attend or exhibit at Interpack if our budget is limited?
output_examples.output: A comparison of attend-only and booth options, with expected costs, pipeline assumptions, qualitative benefits, and a recommendation based on budget constraints.

output_examples.input: Prepare a one-page budget approval summary for CES 2026.
output_examples.output: A concise executive summary with total investment, major cost drivers, expected leads, projected revenue range, risks, and recommended next steps.

best_practices: Provide booth size, team size, city, currency, travel origin, and target customer segment when possible.
Ask the assistant to separate confirmed prices from assumptions and benchmark estimates.
Use conservative conversion assumptions for a first-time show or an untested market.
anti_patterns: Do not treat generated cost estimates as vendor quotes or final organizer pricing.
Do not omit staff time, shipping, insurance, taxes, service fees, or contingency from the budget.
Do not rely on one ROI scenario when leadership needs to understand downside risk.

faq.question: Can this skill calculate trade show ROI?
faq.answer: Yes. It estimates revenue, ROI, cost per lead, and break-even deals from lead and conversion assumptions.

faq.question: Does it work for attend-only trips?
faq.answer: Yes. It can build simpler budgets for registration, travel, lodging, meals, and staff time.

faq.question: Can it compare booth sizes?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide the booth sizes, show name, team size, and goals, then ask for a side-by-side comparison.

faq.question: Are the cost estimates guaranteed?
faq.answer: No. They are planning estimates unless current organizer, hotel, or vendor rates are verified.

faq.question: Can it create a leadership approval summary?
faq.answer: Yes. It can condense the budget and ROI case into an executive summary for approval discussions.

faq.question: Does it require private company data?
faq.answer: No. It can work from rough inputs, but deal size, margin, and conversion rates improve accuracy.
